THE TITLE OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT CONTROL AND THE CAUSE OF ACTION STANDS IF ALLEGATIONS THEREIN STATE ANY CAUSE OF ACTION. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT "EACH COTERMINOUS OWNER IS ENTITLED TO THE LATERAL AND SUBJACENT SUPPORT WHICH HIS LAND RECEIVES FROM THE ADJOINING LAND." "ALL THAT [SECTION 832] DOES IS TO PERMIT A LAND OWNER TO EXCAVATE, FREED FROM THE ABSOLUTE COMMON LAW RIGHT OF LATERAL SUPPORT IN HIS NEIGHBOR, PROVIDED CERTAIN CONDITIONS IN THE CODE SECTION ARE COMPLIED WITH, AND ALWAYS PROVIDED THAT NEGLIGENCE OF THE EXCAVATOR IS NOT THE PROXIMATE CAUSE OF DAMAGE TO THE PROPERTY OF THE ADJOINING LAND OWNER." MARIN MUNICIPAL WATER DIST. V. NORTHWESTERN PAC. R. CO. (1967) 253 CAL.APP.2D 83. EXCAVATION IS NOT AN ISSUE IN THIS CASE, SO THE EXCEPTIONS PROVIDED FOR IN CC 832 ARE NOT APPLICABLE. AT A MINIMUM PLAINTIFF STATED A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AND THE TITLE OF THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION MAY BE DISREGARDED. Reporter: Lisa Ussery; CSR# 11534; 916-223-1747; lisaussery2000@gmail.com

Mr. Radcliffe requests clarification of the courts tentative ruling. Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT MARY COULTON Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant In Support Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings As To Plaintiffs' Third Cause Of Action is DENIED. The title of the cause of action does not control and the cause of action stands if allegations therein state any cause of action. It is undisputed that "Each coterminous owner is entitled to the lateral and subjacent support which his land receives from the adjoining land." "All that [section 832] does is to permit a land owner to excavate, freed from the absolute common law right of lateral support in his neighbor, provided certain conditions in the code section are complied with, and always provided that negligence of the excavator is not the proximate cause of damage to the property of the adjoining land owner." Marin Municipal Water Dist. v. Northwestern Pac. R. Co. (1967) 253 Cal.App.2d 83. Excavation is not an issue in this case, so the exceptions provided for in CC 832 are not applicable. At a minimum Plaintiff stated a cause of action for negligence and the title of the third cause of action may be disregarded.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the court's tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
